page: Amazon Web Services c8gb.2xlarge is a compute-optimized instance built on the Nitro hypervisor. It features an AWS Graviton ARMv9 Neoverse-V2 processor with 8 dedicated physical cores and 8 vCPUs running at 2.8 GHz. The architecture is arm64, with a cache configuration consisting of 64 KB L1d, 2 MB L2, and 36 MB L3 cache. The instance is equipped with 16.0 GB of memory, providing a ratio of 2.0 GB per core. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 17 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates strong single-core performance, with a Geekbench single-core score of 1890.0 and a bzip3 single-threaded decompression rate of 14.48 GB/sec, both placing in the top 25% tier. Text processing performance is also strong at 2337.0. Multi-core and general compute metrics fall within the average range, including a Geekbench multi-core score of 10105.0 and a stress-ng multi-core CPU performance of 13645.12. Memory bandwidth is measured at 924.95 GB/sec for cached small blocks and 154.43 GB/sec for uncached large blocks.

Tradeoffs are evident in cryptographic operations and larger model processing. The Passmark CPU encryption test registers in the bottom 25% at 1506.69, despite hardware support for AES. Additionally, LLM inference for prompt processing on a medium 7B model is poor at 15.52 tokens/sec, though text generation for the same model remains average at 11.46 tokens/sec. From a resource-density perspective, the c8gb.2xlarge offers a dedicated-core ARMv9 architecture that delivers competitive compute density, though the lack of bundled local storage requires external provisioning for storage-heavy workloads. This instance is suited for workloads such as software compilation, static web serving, and single-threaded data decompression.
description: Amazon Web Services c8gb.2xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 8 dedicated physical cores on an arm64 AWS Graviton ARMv9 Neoverse-V2 processor running at 2.8 GHz. It is configured with 16.0 GB of memory, yielding 2.0 GB per core, and operates on the Nitro hypervisor with a baseline network bandwidth of 17 Gbps. The instance lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Performance benchmarks show strong single-core capabilities, including a Geekbench single-core score of 1890.0 and a bzip3 single-threaded decompression rate of 14.48 GB/sec. However, cryptographic performance is a notable tradeoff, with the Passmark encryption test scoring in the bottom 25%. This instance is suited for workloads such as software compilation, static web serving, and lightweight single-threaded processing tasks that benefit from dedicated ARMv9 cores but do not require local SSD storage.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c8gb.2xlarge is an arm64 compute-optimized instance with 8 dedicated Graviton ARMv9 cores, 16 GB RAM, and 17 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering strong single-core performance.

tagline: An arm64 compute-optimized instance featuring dedicated ARMv9 cores and strong single-core performance for compute-intensive workloads without local storage requirements.
bullet_points: 8 dedicated physical cores on an arm64 AWS Graviton ARMv9 Neoverse-V2 processor
16.0 GB of system memory providing 2.0 GB per core
17 Gbps baseline network bandwidth on the Nitro hypervisor
Strong single-core performance in Geekbench and bzip3 decompression tests
Tradeoff in cryptographic performance with a weak Passmark encryption score
Suited for software compilation, static web serving, and text processing
